chunks.title: Components & Initial Placement
chunks.content:
## Components & Initial Placement

### Per-Clan Components
Each player takes:
- 1 Clan Sheet + 1 Valhalla Sheet
- 8 Warriors (STR 1), 1 Leader (STR 3), 1 Ship (STR 0)
- Clan tokens for Rage, Axes, Horns, Glory

### Starting Stats (all clans identical)
| Stat | Starting Value |
|------|----------------|
| Rage | 6 |
| Axes | 3 |
| Horns | 4 |
| Glory | 0 |

### Board Setup
1. Place board in center
2. Place one Pillage token (green border, reward side up) on Yggdrasil (center province)
3. Shuffle remaining 8 Pillage tokens; place one face-up on each outer province
4. Place Ragnarök tokens on Ragnarök Track (one province destroyed per Age)
5. Randomly determine First Player; give First Player token

chunks.title: Reserve Placement & Gods' Gifts Draft
chunks.content:
## Reserve Placement & Gods' Gifts Draft

### Starting Reserve
- Place figures in reserve up to your **Horns limit (4)**
- Default starting reserve: Leader (1) + Ship (1) + 2 Warriors = 4 figures

### Gods' Gifts Draft (start of each Age)
1. Deal **8 cards** from the current Age deck to each player
2. Each player selects **1 card**, places it face down, passes remaining 7 to the **LEFT**
3. Repeat until each player has drafted **6 cards**
4. The final 2 cards per player are discarded unseen

> Battle Cards are **not** drafted — each player holds their own separate personal hand of Battle Cards.

chunks.title: Action Phase — Turn Actions
chunks.content:
## Action Phase — Turn Actions

At the start of the Action Phase, all players reset **Rage** to their current Rage stat. On your turn, choose **ONE** action. When Rage = 0, you must Pass.

| Action | Rage Cost | Details |
|---|---|---|
| **Invade** | = figure's STR (min 1) | Place 1 figure from reserve into any empty village in an outer province, or into your fjord (Ship only). Ships have STR 0 but cost 1 Rage. Cannot invade Yggdrasil directly. |
| **March** | 1 | Pick one province. Move any/all your figures from it to empty villages in one other province. Triggers battle if enemies are present. |
| **Upgrade** | 0 (most) | Play an Upgrade card from hand onto your Clan Sheet. Apply effect immediately. Monster cards add that Monster figure to your reserve. |
| **Quest** | 0 | Play a Quest card face down onto your Clan Sheet quest slot. One active quest at a time. Scored at end of Age in Quest Phase. |
| **Pillage** | 0 | Declare a province where you have ≥1 figure. Opponents may contest by spending Rage to Invade. Battle resolves if contested. Win = gain Pillage token reward. |
| **Pass** | 0 | End your turn. Action Phase ends when all players pass or hit 0 Rage. |

chunks.title: Battle Resolution
chunks.content:
## Battle Resolution

Triggered during **Invade**, **March**, or **Pillage** when enemy figures are present.

### Steps
1. Each involved player secretly selects 1 Battle Card from hand, places face down
2. All players reveal simultaneously
3. Each player totals: **STR of all figures in province + Battle Card STR bonus**
4. **Highest total wins** → winner gains Glory equal to their **Axes** stat
5. **Tie** → ALL tied players lose (figures go to Valhalla; no Axes Glory awarded)
6. **Losers** → all figures in the province sent to Valhalla; no base Glory from losing
7. All participants discard their played Battle Card

> Used Battle Cards are gone for the Age — they do not refresh until the next Age's draft.

chunks.title: Card Types Overview
chunks.content:
## Card Types Overview

| Card Type | Played Where | Persists? | Primary Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Upgrade** | Clan Sheet slot | Yes — permanent | Boost stats (Rage/Axes/Horns) or grant special abilities including combat triggers |
| **Monster** (Upgrade subtype) | Clan Sheet + reserve | Yes — permanent | Adds Monster figure (STR 4–6) + printed special ability |
| **Quest** | Hand (secret) | Yes — revealed at scoring | Secret objective; scored at end of Age if conditions met |
| **Battle** | Personal hand | No — discarded after use | Played face-down each battle; provides STR bonus or a special effect |

### Upgrade Card Slots on Clan Sheet
- **Warrior upgrades** — boost STR of all Warrior figures
- **Leader upgrade** — improves Leader figure
- **Ship upgrade** — improves Ship figure
- **Clan upgrades** — raise Rage, Axes, or Horns stats

chunks.title: Gods' Gifts Draft — Playing Drafted Cards
chunks.content:
## Gods' Gifts Draft — Playing Drafted Cards

### Playing Cards After the Draft
- **Upgrade / Monster cards** — played during the Action Phase as the Upgrade action (0 Rage for most; apply effect immediately)
- **Quest cards** — kept secret in hand; never "played" as an action, they are revealed automatically at end-of-Age Quest Phase if their condition is met
- **Battle Cards** — not drafted; held in your separate personal hand and played during battles

### Key Reminders
- Cards already played on your Clan Sheet (Upgrades) **remain** there through the Discard Phase — only unplayed hand cards are discarded
- Quest cards are discarded after scoring whether or not they were fulfilled
- Monster figures added to reserve count against your **Horns** limit

chunks.title: Combat Card Resolution Order
chunks.content:
## Combat Card Resolution Order

The sequence in which card effects fire during a battle:

| Step | What Happens | Card Effects That Fire |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Battle announced | Effects worded **"when battle is announced"** trigger now |
| 2 | Players secretly select 1 Battle Card | — |
| 3 | All Battle Cards revealed simultaneously | Effects worded **"before STR calculation"** or **"before battle"** trigger now |
| 4 | STR totals calculated | **Passive Upgrade abilities** are always-on — they modify base STR automatically and do not "trigger" |
| 5 | Outcome-altering effects apply | Battle Card or Upgrade effects that say **"highest STR loses instead"** or similar resolve here, after totals are known |
| 6 | Winner determined; Axes Glory awarded | — |
| 7 | Loser figures sent to Valhalla | — |
| 8 | Post-battle effects fire | Effects worded **"after battle"** or **"after result"** trigger now |

### Priority Rules for Simultaneous Effects
- **Simultaneous triggers** resolve in **clockwise order** starting from the player who initiated the battle (the Pillage declarer or Invading/Marching player)
- **Conflicting outcome-change effects** (two players both trying to flip the result): player with the **lower Axes stat** resolves first *(verify against official FAQ)*
- **Monster abilities**: check each card for its timing keyword ("before battle", "during STR calculation", "after battle") and slot into the matching step above

chunks.title: Ongoing VP Sources (Glory Track)
chunks.content:
## Ongoing VP Sources

All Glory is tracked live on the shared Glory Track — there is no end-game calculation.

| Source | Glory Earned | When |
|---|---|---|
| Win a battle | = your current **Axes** stat | Immediately on winning |
| Figure consumed by Ragnarök | +1 per figure destroyed | Ragnarök Phase each Age |
| Fulfill a Quest | Amount on Quest card | Quest Phase each Age |
| Pillage a province | Amount on Pillage token | When pillage succeeds |
| Card effects | As stated on card | Per card timing |

chunks.title: End-Game Winner & Tiebreakers
chunks.content:
## End-Game (After Age III Release Valhalla Phase)

Player with the **most Glory** on the Glory Track wins.

### Tiebreakers (in order)
1. Highest **Rage** stat
2. Highest **Axes** stat
3. Share the victory

chunks.title: Strategic Tips for First-Time Players
chunks.content:
## Strategic Tips for First-Time Players

### Draft (Gods' Gifts)
- **Draft for synergy, not isolated power.** A Quest card is worthless without the figures or actions to complete it. Commit to a strategy in the first 2–3 picks and draft to reinforce it.
- **Deny powerful combos.** If a card doesn't fit your plan but would supercharge an opponent's, consider drafting it purely to block them.
- **Upgrades you pass are gone.** Don't assume you can grab a key Clan Upgrade next round — if you skip it in Age I, assume you'll never see it again.

### Rage & Actions
- **Rage is your budget — spend it deliberately.** Overcommitting to provinces early leaves you unable to react when opponents invade or Ragnarök hits.
- **Ragnarök death is free Glory.** Before each Age's Ragnarök phase, park figures in the doomed province intentionally — you earn 1 Glory per figure consumed. Dying in Ragnarök beats dying in a failed battle.

### Battle & Battle Cards
- **Your Battle Card hand is finite and does not reset mid-Age.** Burning cards in early skirmishes leaves you defenseless in decisive late-Age battles.
- **Low Axes clans should avoid combat.** Glory from winning scales with your Axes stat. If yours is low, prioritize Pillage and Quests over fighting.

### Figures & Deployment
- **Dead figures return at end of each Age.** Don't over-value keeping figures alive — what matters is *where* they die. Valhalla is a revolving door, not a graveyard.
- **Monsters eat Horns capacity.** A Monster in reserve counts against your Horns limit, shrinking your deployable force. Only recruit Monsters when you have a clear plan to deploy them.

chunks.title: 5th Player Expansion
chunks.content:
## 5th Player Expansion

Extends the base game (2–4 players) to support **5 players**.

- Adds a 5th clan with its own clan sheet, figures, and Age cards
- Board and province village counts adjusted to accommodate 5 players
- Draft and Age structure remain the same as the base game

> Full setup adjustments are in the expansion insert; the base rulebook covers 2–4 players only.

chunks.title: Gods of Asgard Expansion
chunks.content:
## Gods of Asgard Expansion

Adds large **God figures** (e.g., Thor, Odin, Loki) that roam provinces and influence battles.

- God figures occupy provinces and interact with clan figures during battles in their province
- Each God has a unique ability that triggers during or after combat
- Gods can be invoked by players using expansion-specific cards or actions

> Individual God STR values, invocation costs, and Ragnarök interactions require the expansion rulebook.

chunks.title: Mystics of Midgard Expansion
chunks.content:
## Mystics of Midgard Expansion

Adds **Mystic figures** to each clan with unique special abilities.

- Each clan gains one or more Mystic figures with passive or active abilities
- Mystics are recruited and deployed like standard figures but interact with expansion-specific mechanics
- Abilities vary per clan and may affect battles, pillaging, or quests

> Individual Mystic ability text, recruitment costs, and whether Mystics count toward Horns limits are on expansion cards only.
